chief_complaint: A 69-year-old woman developed acute-onset chest pain, tightness, and dyspnea on postoperative day 11 following open reduction and internal fixation of a left humeral shaft fracture.
history: The patient reported significant anxiety due to the acute and unexplained nature of her symptoms. She had a history of long-term steroid therapy and a history of cancer.
exam: Vital signs: not specified. Physical examination findings: left upper limb swelling.
labs: Doppler ultrasound: hypoechoic thrombus in the left brachial vein. Computed tomographic pulmonary angiography: bilateral pulmonary artery filling defects.
correct_diagnosis: Acute postoperative upper extremity deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolus
differentials: Acute postoperative upper extremity deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolus
correct_ranking: Acute postoperative upper extremity deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolus
